meta.building: Flèche de la cathédrale Notre-Dame de Paris
meta.date_built: spire designed/erected 1859 (Viollet-le-Duc); lost in the fire 15 Apr 2019; rebuilt à l'identique, cathedral reopened 7–8 Dec 2024; new spire summit + rooster 2023

components.role: The timber stool transferring the spire's load onto the four crossing piers.
components.note: single medium-confidence footprint source → conjecture (cross-check only)

components.role: Octagonal souche of the flèche, lead over an oak armature.
components.note: octagonal form + proportion from the VLD PD 'Flèche' plate; absolute height scaled off the plate × the measured 66 m extent

components.role: Octagonal fut of the flèche, lead over an oak armature.

components.role: The tapering needle rising to the rooster — the silhouette's final stroke.
components.note: octagonal form + proportion from the VLD PD 'Flèche' plate; absolute height scaled off the plate × the measured 66 m extent

components.role: Horizontal ring beam tying the eight oak arêtes into the octagon at this level.
components.note: Octagon radius from the ad quadratum rule; exact springing dimension is a GAP (rule_derived, not surveyed).

Pinnacle set out by the 1486 Roriczer rule — base square, inscribed squares at 45°.
components.note: Shaft height = base × 6 (Roriczer vertical multiple) — CITED-UNCERTAIN, re-verify against the verbatim 1847 text.

components.role: Tapering cap of the pinnacle — its vertical multiple (~7×) is cited-uncertain.
components.note: Auszug height from the ~7× Roriczer multiple — cited-uncertain, so flagged conjecture (uncertainty propagates).

components.role: Winged evangelist symbol leading an apostle group up the base of the spire.
components.note: Count, heights and arrangement are measured (PERSEE-2009 + corroboration); the figurative massing is stylized, not a sculpture reconstruction. Weights cited-uncertain (see canonical).

components.role: Copper apostle climbing the base of the flèche toward heaven.
Gilt orb at the foot of the rooster, capping the needle.
The gilded copper rooster at the very summit — a reliquary holding three relics. Position from the measured 96 m total height; its published weight (~30 vs ~10 kg) is cited-uncertain.
components.note: Single topmost component, at the measured 96 m. Weight cited-uncertain (V13 → conjecture).
